page: Amazon Web Services r8id.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors on the x86_64 architecture. It features 384 vCPUs, corresponding to 192 physical cores with hyperthreading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processing unit is supported by a 480 MB L3 cache and includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix and integer operations.

This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and is equipped with 3072 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled as 22,800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with uncached read-only memory bandwidth reaching 1124.8 GB/sec. The server demonstrates exceptional database capabilities, scoring in the top 10% for Passmark database operations and achieving 451.6 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speeds remain average to poor due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

Given its massive DDR5 memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high-bandwidth networking, the r8id.96xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for enterprise workloads. It is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, data-intensive ETL pipelines, and heavy database operations where local storage speed and memory capacity are critical bottlenecks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.96xlarge is a memory-optimized, high-capacity server designed for data-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 384 vCPUs and 3072 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. The instance features 22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database execution speeds, including 451.6 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. Lacking dedicated GPUs, its strength lies in memory-bound and storage-heavy operations rather than parallel graphics acceleration. This hardware configuration is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, real-time analytics, and high-throughput transactional systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 384 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 384 Intel vCPUs, 3 TB DDR5 RAM, and 22.8 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage platform featuring 384 vCPUs, 3 TB DDR5 RAM, and 22.8 TB of local NVMe SSDs.
bullet_points: 384 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
3072 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and large-scale data processing
